result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make it so that every person genuinely understands their own mind—why they believe what they believe, why they fear what they fear, why they hurt others. Not through some imposed uniformity, but through a clarity that arrives like waking up from a dream you didn't know you were in.

The cruelty, the waste, the wars fought by people who never chose the grievance, the loneliness of people who can't name what they need—so much of it seems to come from this fog. We are the most intelligent species we know of, yet we stumble through our own interiors like strangers in a dark house, breaking things we can't see.

If that fog lifted—not perfectly, not permanently, but enough that listening became easier than defending, that curiosity became stronger than pride—I think the rest would start to sort itself. Not utopia. Still conflict, still grief, still the hard work of living. But at least we'd be fighting the real problems, rather than the shadows we project onto each other.

What about you? If you had that lever, what would you move?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
